description: Sample test set in Ragas format derived from legacy evaluation system queries covering various Earth science domains and NASA platforms/instruments

test_cases.user_input: What datasets are available for studying agricultural crop health and soil moisture monitoring?
reference_contexts: NASA provides multiple satellite missions for agricultural monitoring including MODIS vegetation indices, SMAP soil moisture measurements, and Landsat multispectral imagery.
Collections include SMAP/Sentinel-1 L2 Radiometer/Radar 30-Second Scene 3 km EASE-Grid Soil Moisture for soil moisture, MODIS/Terra+Aqua vegetation indices for crop health monitoring.
Variables: Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (NDVI), Enhanced Vegetation Index (EVI), Soil Moisture Active Passive (SMAP) measurements, leaf area index.
test_cases.reference: NASA Earthdata provides comprehensive agricultural datasets including SMAP soil moisture products, MODIS vegetation indices (NDVI, EVI), and Landsat imagery for monitoring crop health, soil conditions, and agricultural land use patterns.

test_cases.user_input: Find satellite data for monitoring forestry and tree management practices
reference_contexts: Forest monitoring datasets include GEDI lidar measurements for forest structure, ICESat-2 canopy height, MODIS land cover products, and Landsat time-series for deforestation tracking.
Global Forest Cover Change products derived from Landsat provide annual forest extent and change detection capabilities.
Variables: tree height, canopy cover, biomass, forest type classification, deforestation rates.
test_cases.reference: NASA provides forest monitoring datasets from GEDI spaceborne lidar, ICESat-2 canopy measurements, MODIS land cover products, and Landsat-based forest change detection products for sustainable forestry management.

test_cases.user_input: What atmospheric aerosol and air quality measurements are available?
reference_contexts: Aura OMI and MODIS instruments provide aerosol optical depth measurements globally. CALIPSO offers vertical profiles of aerosol distribution.
Collections: OMI/Aura Multi-wavelength Aerosol Optical Depth, MODIS Aerosol 5-Min L2 Swath products, CALIPSO lidar aerosol profiles.
Variables: Aerosol Optical Depth (AOD), Angstrom Exponent, PM2.5, aerosol type classification, vertical extinction profiles.
test_cases.reference: NASA Earthdata offers aerosol and air quality measurements from Aura OMI, MODIS, and CALIPSO instruments providing aerosol optical depth, particulate matter concentrations, and vertical aerosol profiles for air quality monitoring.

test_cases.user_input: Show me datasets measuring atmospheric water vapor and precipitation patterns
reference_contexts: GPM (Global Precipitation Measurement) mission provides global precipitation estimates every 3 hours. AIRS measures atmospheric water vapor profiles.
Collections: GPM IMERG precipitation products, AIRS/Aqua atmospheric water vapor, MODIS precipitable water vapor, TRMM rainfall estimates.
Variables: precipitation rate, total column water vapor, relative humidity profiles, rainfall accumulation.
test_cases.reference: NASA provides atmospheric water vapor and precipitation datasets from GPM, AIRS, and MODIS including global precipitation rates, water vapor columns, and humidity profiles for weather and climate studies.

test_cases.user_input: Find cloud properties and atmospheric radiation budget data
reference_contexts: CERES instruments measure Earth's radiation budget. MODIS and CloudSat provide comprehensive cloud properties.
Collections: CERES Energy Balanced and Filled (EBAF) products, MODIS Cloud Product, CloudSat Cloud Profiling Radar, MISR cloud heights.
Variables: cloud fraction, cloud optical thickness, cloud top temperature/pressure, longwave and shortwave radiation fluxes, albedo.
test_cases.reference: NASA Earthdata includes cloud and radiation datasets from CERES, MODIS, CloudSat, and MISR providing cloud properties, radiation budget components, and Earth's energy balance measurements.

test_cases.user_input: What glacier and ice sheet monitoring data is available from Greenland and Antarctica?
reference_contexts: ICESat and ICESat-2 provide ice sheet elevation changes. Operation IceBridge fills the gap between missions. GRACE measures ice mass changes.
Collections: ICESat-2 ATL06 Land Ice Height, GRACE ice mass anomalies, MEaSUREs Greenland/Antarctic ice velocity, IceBridge elevation surveys.
Variables: ice surface elevation, ice velocity, mass balance, ice thickness, glacier terminus position.
test_cases.reference: NASA provides comprehensive glacier and ice sheet monitoring data from ICESat-2 laser altimetry, GRACE mass change measurements, and Operation IceBridge surveys covering Greenland and Antarctic ice sheets.

test_cases.user_input: Find sea ice extent and thickness measurements for polar regions
reference_contexts: AMSR-E/AMSR2 passive microwave sensors provide daily sea ice concentration. ICESat-2 measures sea ice thickness. NSIDC produces standardized sea ice extent products.
Collections: AMSR2 Sea Ice Concentration, ICESat-2 ATL07 Sea Ice Height, NSIDC Sea Ice Index, CryoSat-2 sea ice thickness.
Variables: sea ice concentration, ice thickness, ice extent, freeboard height, ice type (first-year vs multi-year).
test_cases.reference: NASA Earthdata offers sea ice monitoring datasets from AMSR2, ICESat-2, and CryoSat-2 providing daily sea ice extent, concentration, and thickness measurements for Arctic and Antarctic regions.

test_cases.user_input: Show me permafrost and frozen ground monitoring datasets
reference_contexts: SMAP provides soil freeze/thaw state detection. Land surface temperature from MODIS indicates permafrost thermal state. In-situ networks provide ground temperature data.
Collections: SMAP L3 Radiometer Northern Hemisphere Daily Freeze/Thaw State, MODIS Land Surface Temperature, permafrost monitoring networks.
Variables: freeze/thaw state, active layer thickness, soil temperature profiles, ground thermal regime, permafrost extent.
test_cases.reference: NASA provides permafrost monitoring data from SMAP freeze/thaw detection, MODIS land surface temperature, and integrated in-situ measurements tracking permafrost extent and thermal state in Arctic regions.

test_cases.user_input: What ocean temperature and salinity measurements are available globally?
reference_contexts: MODIS Aqua provides sea surface temperature. Aquarius and SMAP measure ocean salinity. Argo floats provide subsurface profiles integrated into NASA datasets.
Collections: MODIS Aqua Sea Surface Temperature, Aquarius Sea Surface Salinity, SMAP Sea Surface Salinity, GHRSST Level 4 SST.
Variables: sea surface temperature, sea surface salinity, ocean heat content, mixed layer depth, temperature/salinity profiles.
test_cases.reference: NASA Earthdata includes global ocean temperature from MODIS and GHRSST, and salinity measurements from Aquarius and SMAP satellites, along with integrated Argo float profile data.

test_cases.user_input: Find datasets for studying ocean circulation patterns and currents
reference_contexts: Jason-3 and Sentinel-6 altimetry measure sea surface height for geostrophic currents. OSCAR provides surface current velocities. Ocean color from MODIS tracks circulation patterns.
Collections: Jason-3 Sea Surface Height, OSCAR Ocean Surface Currents, MODIS Ocean Color, AVISO gridded altimetry products.
Variables: sea surface height anomaly, geostrophic currents, ocean surface velocity, sea level anomaly, dynamic topography.
test_cases.reference: NASA provides ocean circulation datasets from Jason-3/Sentinel-6 altimetry, OSCAR surface current products, and MODIS ocean color for studying global circulation patterns and current systems.

test_cases.user_input: What coastal zone and marine geophysics data is available?
reference_contexts: Landsat and Sentinel provide coastal land/water boundaries. MODIS ocean color tracks coastal water quality. Altimetry provides coastal sea level.
Collections: Landsat coastal monitoring, MODIS Aqua ocean color for coastal waters, coastal sea level from altimetry missions, VIIRS coastal imagery.
Variables: coastal erosion, water quality parameters, bathymetry, shoreline position, suspended sediment, coastal vegetation.
test_cases.reference: NASA Earthdata offers coastal monitoring datasets from Landsat, MODIS ocean color, and altimetry missions providing coastal zone changes, water quality, and nearshore processes.

test_cases.user_input: Show me bathymetry and seafloor topography datasets
reference_contexts: Satellite altimetry-derived bathymetry products available. ICESat-2 provides shallow water bathymetry. Global Multi-Resolution Topography synthesis includes NASA data.
Collections: GMRT (Global Multi-Resolution Topography), ICESat-2 ATL03 shallow bathymetry, satellite-derived bathymetry from altimetry.
Variables: ocean depth, seafloor elevation, bathymetric slope, submarine features, coastal bathymetry.
test_cases.reference: NASA contributes to global bathymetry through satellite altimetry-derived products and ICESat-2 shallow water measurements integrated into comprehensive seafloor topography datasets.

test_cases.user_input: What ice core and paleoclimate proxy records are available?
reference_contexts: NASA archives ice core data from Greenland and Antarctic projects. Paleoclimate data synthesis includes proxy records. NSIDC hosts comprehensive paleoclimate datasets.
Collections: Antarctic ice core records (EPICA, Law Dome), Greenland ice cores (GISP2, GRIP), lake sediment cores, tree ring data.
Variables: δ18O isotopes, CO2 concentration, methane levels, temperature proxies, accumulation rates, dust concentrations.
test_cases.reference: NASA Earthdata archives ice core records from Antarctic and Greenland drilling projects along with paleoclimate proxy datasets including isotope ratios, greenhouse gases, and temperature reconstructions.

test_cases.user_input: Find datasets for monitoring wildfires, hurricanes, and extreme weather events
reference_contexts: MODIS and VIIRS detect active fires. GOES and GPM track hurricanes. Lightning Imaging Sensor monitors storm intensity.
Collections: MODIS/VIIRS Active Fire products, GPM hurricane precipitation, GOES-R Advanced Baseline Imager, LIS Lightning flashes, TRMM cyclone data.
Variables: fire radiative power, burned area, hurricane track/intensity, precipitation rates, lightning flash rate, extreme precipitation.
test_cases.reference: NASA provides natural disaster monitoring through MODIS/VIIRS fire detection, GPM hurricane tracking, GOES storm imagery, and Lightning Imaging Sensor data for wildfire, hurricane, and severe weather events.

test_cases.user_input: Show me OPERA surface water extent and surface disturbance products
reference_contexts: OPERA (Observational Products for End-Users from Remote Sensing Analysis) produces near-real-time surface water and disturbance maps from Sentinel and Landsat data.
Collections: OPERA DSWx (Dynamic Surface Water Extent), OPERA DIST (Surface Disturbance), distributed through NASA PO.DAAC and LP DAAC.
Variables: water extent classification, water confidence, disturbance indication, change detection, land cover transitions.
test_cases.reference: NASA OPERA provides near-real-time surface water extent (DSWx) and surface disturbance (DIST) products derived from Sentinel and Landsat imagery for monitoring water bodies and land changes.

test_cases.user_input: What MODIS vegetation and land cover datasets are available?
reference_contexts: MODIS on Terra and Aqua satellites provides 16-day NDVI/EVI composites, annual land cover classification, and leaf area index products.
Collections: MOD13 NDVI/EVI Vegetation Indices, MCD12 Land Cover Type, MOD15 LAI/FPAR, MOD44 Vegetation Continuous Fields.
Variables: NDVI, EVI, LAI (Leaf Area Index), FPAR, land cover type, vegetation continuous fields, phenology.
test_cases.reference: NASA MODIS provides comprehensive vegetation datasets including NDVI/EVI indices, annual land cover classification, leaf area index, and vegetation continuous fields from Terra and Aqua satellites.

test_cases.user_input: Find ICESat ice sheet elevation and mass balance measurements
reference_contexts: ICESat and ICESat-2 laser altimetry missions measure ice sheet surface elevation. GLAS instrument on ICESat and ATLAS on ICESat-2 provide precise elevation data.
Collections: ICESat-2 ATL06 Land Ice Height, ICESat GLAS elevation products, derived mass balance estimates for Greenland and Antarctica.
Variables: ice surface elevation, elevation change rates, ice sheet mass balance, surface slope, roughness.
test_cases.reference: NASA ICESat and ICESat-2 missions provide precise laser altimetry measurements of ice sheet elevation, elevation change rates, and mass balance for Greenland and Antarctic ice sheets.

test_cases.user_input: What Aura atmospheric chemistry and ozone data is available?
reference_contexts: Aura satellite carries OMI, MLS, TES, and HIRDLS instruments measuring atmospheric composition. OMI provides ozone column amounts and profiles.
Collections: OMI Total Column Ozone, MLS atmospheric composition profiles, TES tropospheric ozone, OMI NO2, SO2, aerosols.
Variables: total column ozone, ozone profiles, NO2, SO2, CO, formaldehyde, aerosol index, trace gas concentrations.
test_cases.reference: NASA Aura satellite provides atmospheric chemistry datasets including ozone measurements from OMI, trace gas profiles from MLS, and air quality products measuring NO2, SO2, and other pollutants.

test_cases.user_input: Show me MISR multi-angle aerosol and cloud datasets
reference_contexts: MISR (Multi-angle Imaging SpectroRadiometer) on Terra captures imagery from 9 different angles enabling 3D characterization of aerosols and clouds.
Collections: MISR Level 2 Aerosol Product, MISR Cloud Product, MISR Ancillary Geographic Product, stereo height retrievals.
Variables: aerosol optical depth, aerosol type, cloud top height, cloud motion vectors, particle shape, angular reflectance.
test_cases.reference: NASA MISR instrument provides unique multi-angle observations enabling 3D characterization of aerosols and clouds including aerosol optical depth, particle properties, and cloud heights.

test_cases.user_input: Find AMSR microwave radiometer sea ice and ocean measurements
reference_contexts: AMSR-E (on Aqua) and AMSR2 (on GCOM-W1) are passive microwave radiometers measuring sea ice, ocean parameters, and soil moisture through clouds.
Collections: AMSR2 Sea Ice Concentration, AMSR-E/AMSR2 Sea Surface Temperature, Ocean Surface Wind Speed, Rain Rate, Snow Water Equivalent.
Variables: sea ice concentration, sea surface temperature, wind speed, columnar water vapor, cloud liquid water, precipitation rate.
test_cases.reference: NASA AMSR-E and AMSR2 passive microwave radiometers provide all-weather measurements of sea ice concentration, sea surface temperature, ocean wind speed, and precipitation for polar and ocean monitoring.
